_meta.note: ANONYMIZED, English, k>=3-org distilled patterns from Binclusive's dynamic-audit corpus. Failure-shapes clustered offline by LLM (frozen in data/clusters/), gated k>=3-distinct-org and anonymized by deterministic code. Contains NO customer identifiers (org/project ids, elements, urls stripped; frequency as tier only). Regenerate by re-running the distiller.

patterns.failureShape: A form input (search box, text field, combobox) has no visible, programmatically associated label and relies on a placeholder for its name, so the label disappears on input and is unreliable for assistive tech.
patterns.fix: Give every input a persistent visible <label> associated by id, or an aria-label when a visible label isn't possible. Never use placeholder text as the only label.

patterns.failureShape: An input with a format or length constraint (a 9-digit account number, a phone number split into single-digit boxes) provides no instructions or per-field labels, so users don't know the required format and error correction is hard.
patterns.fix: Provide visible instructions and constraints for the input (expected format, length), label each sub-field if the input is split, and prefer a single field that accepts the whole value to support autofill and easy correction.
